_note: Mock evaluation result. Input was the canonical_declaration.txt above. Evaluated against all four constitutional laws.

candidateEvaluations.reason: Audit-only action is fully admissible. Feasibility law satisfied: the planned schedule is internally coherent from 5am to 10pm — total declared blocks sum to exactly 17 hours. Observability law satisfied: the deviation (11pm actual vs 10pm target) is explicitly declared. Constraint satisfaction law satisfied: audit action proposes no changes that could violate anchor blocks. Robustness law satisfied: high margin — audit introduces zero corrective risk.

Audit-plus-minimal-correction is admissible. Feasibility law satisfied: the 60-minute slip can be recovered by compressing the reading/admin block (no declared commitment) from 60m to 0m, moving bedtime from 11pm to 10pm without touching any anchor block. Observability law satisfied: the fix is measurable and directly targets the declared deviation. Constraint satisfaction law satisfied: wake time, work hours, gym block, and sleep minimum are all preserved. Robustness law satisfied: compression of an uncommitted block is the smallest possible change.
adjustments: Compress or eliminate the 9pm reading/admin block (60m, no declared commitment).
Bedtime advances from 11pm to 10pm — sleep minimum of 7h is preserved (10pm to 5am).
All anchor blocks remain untouched: wake 5am, work 8am-5pm, gym 6pm-7:30pm.

candidateEvaluations.reason: Rule derivation is admissible for this case because the deviation pattern is clearly declared and consistent. Feasibility law satisfied. Observability law satisfied: the deviation source (dinner/wind-down expansion into reading block) is described as a recurring pattern. Robustness law satisfied: the derived rule is grounded in declared behavior, not inferred from missing data. Derived rule: evening blocks after 7:30pm must be treated as compressible by default — do not schedule committed tasks after 8:30pm.
adjustments: Derived rule: all post-7:30pm blocks are soft by default unless explicitly declared as committed.
Apply this rule: reading/admin after 9pm is compressible in all future schedule revisions.
Anchor all committed evening activity before 8:30pm to prevent cascading delay.

notes: All three candidates are LAWFUL — schedule problem is well-specified and deterministic.
Best candidate is B: eliminates the 60-minute slip by compressing the reading block, which has no declared commitment.
Candidate C produces a reusable rule (post-7:30pm blocks are soft) that would apply to future schedule versions.
Sleep minimum (7h, 10pm–5am) is satisfied under Candidate B correction.
The dinner/wind-down overrun is the root cause but is not directly compressible without touching an anchor block — the reading block absorbs the drift instead.
Evaluation ran against the confirmed canonical declaration, not raw input.
